; Test case 2: smin intrinsic with known sign bits
; RUN: opt -passes=slp-vectorizer -S -o -

define void @test_smin_minbitwidth(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c, i32 %d) {
entry:
  %a1 = ashr i32 %a, 16  ; Guaranteed to have 16 sign bits
  %b1 = ashr i32 %b, 16
  %c1 = ashr i32 %c, 16
  %d1 = ashr i32 %d, 16
  
  %x1 = add i32 %a1, 100
  %x2 = add i32 %b1, 200
  %x3 = add i32 %c1, 300
  %x4 = add i32 %d1, 400
  
  %min1 = call i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32 %x1, i32 32767)
  %min2 = call i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32 %x2, i32 32767)
  %min3 = call i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32 %x3, i32 32767)
  %min4 = call i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32 %x4, i32 32767)
  
  store i32 %min1, i32* null
  store i32 %min2, i32* null
  store i32 %min3, i32* null
  store i32 %min4, i32* null
  ret void
}

; Test case 3: umax intrinsic with known high bits zero
; RUN: opt -passes=slp-vectorizer -S -o -

define void @test_umax_minbitwidth(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c, i32 %d) {
entry:
  %a1 = and i32 %a, 65535  ; Only lower 16 bits can be set
  %b1 = and i32 %b, 65535
  %c1 = and i32 %c, 65535
  %d1 = and i32 %d, 65535
  
  %max1 = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %a1, i32 1000)
  %max2 = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %b1, i32 1000)
  %max3 = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %c1, i32 1000)
  %max4 = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %d1, i32 1000)
  
  store i32 %max1, i32* null
  store i32 %max2, i32* null
  store i32 %max3, i32* null
  store i32 %max4, i32* null
  ret void
}

; Test case 4: Mixed intrinsics to test type consistency
; RUN: opt -passes=slp-vectorizer -S -o -

define void @test_mixed_intrinsics(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c, i32 %d) {
entry:
  %a1 = and i32 %a, 32767  ; 15 bits max
  %b1 = and i32 %b, 32767
  %c1 = and i32 %c, 32767
  %d1 = and i32 %d, 32767
  
  ; These should all be reducible to 16-bit operations
  %abs1 = call i32 @llvm.abs.i32(i32 %a1, i1 true)
  %smin1 = call i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32 %b1, i32 10000)
  %smax1 = call i32 @llvm.smax.i32(i32 %c1, i32 -10000)
  %umax1 = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %d1, i32 5000)
  
  store i32 %abs1, i32* null
  store i32 %smin1, i32* null
  store i32 %smax1, i32* null
  store i32 %umax1, i32* null
  ret void
}

declare i32 @llvm.umin.i32(i32, i32)

", "thoughts": "Verifying with opt args: -passes=slp-vectorizer"}
report:
Looking at this patch, I can identify several issues related to the minbitwidth analysis for abs/smin/smax/umin/umax intrinsics:

## Issues Found:

1. **Missing handling for `Intrinsic::abs` in `CallChecker` logic**: The patch adds support for `abs` intrinsic but doesn't provide a `CallChecker` function for it in the `collectValuesToDemote` function.

2. **Inconsistent bitwidth handling in `vectorizeTree`**: When `It != MinBWs.end()`, the code creates an `IntCast` to `VecTy`, but `VecTy` might not have the reduced bitwidth - it's the original vector type.

3. **Potential type mismatch in `buildIntrinsicArgTypes`**: When `MinBW > 0`, it creates vector types with `MinBW` elements, but this might not match the actual argument types needed.

## Test Cases:

Here are test cases to expose these issues:

```llvm
; Test case 1: abs intrinsic with sign bit analysis
; RUN: opt -passes=slp-vectorizer -S -o -

define void @test_abs_minbitwidth(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c, i32 %d) {
entry:
  %a1 = and i32 %a, 2147483647  ; Clear sign bit
  %b1 = and i32 %b, 2147483647
  %c1 = and i32 %c, 2147483647
  %d1 = and i32 %d, 2147483647
  
  %abs1 = call i32 @llvm.abs.i32(i32 %a1, i1 false)
  %abs2 = call i32 @llvm.abs.i32(i32 %b1, i1 false)
  %abs3 = call i32 @llvm.abs.i32(i32 %c1, i1 false)
  %abs4 = call i32 @llvm.abs.i32(i32 %d1, i1 false)
  
  store i32 %abs1, i32* null
  store i32 %abs2, i32* null
  store i32 %abs3, i32* null
  store i32 %abs4, i32* null
  ret void
}

; Test case 2: smin intrinsic with known sign bits
; RUN: opt -passes=slp-vectorizer -S -o -

define void @test_smin_minbitwidth(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c, i32 %d) {
entry:
  %a1 = ashr i32 %a, 16  ; Guaranteed to have 16 sign bits
  %b1 = ashr i32 %b, 16
  %c1 = ashr i32 %c, 16
  %d1 = ashr i32 %d, 16
  
  %x1 = add i32 %a1, 100
  %x2 = add i32 %b1, 200
  %x3 = add i32 %c1, 300
  %x4 = add i32 %d1, 400
  
  %min1 = call i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32 %x1, i32 32767)
  %min2 = call i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32 %x2, i32 32767)
  %min3 = call i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32 %x3, i32 32767)
  %min4 = call i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32 %x4, i32 32767)
  
  store i32 %min1, i32* null
  store i32 %min2, i32* null
  store i32 %min3, i32* null
  store i32 %min4, i32* null
  ret void
}

; Test case 3: umax intrinsic with known high bits zero
; RUN: opt -passes=slp-vectorizer -S -o -

define void @test_umax_minbitwidth(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c, i32 %d) {
entry:
  %a1 = and i32 %a, 65535  ; Only lower 16 bits can be set
  %b1 = and i32 %b, 65535
  %c1 = and i32 %c, 65535
  %d1 = and i32 %d, 65535
  
  %max1 = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %a1, i32 1000)
  %max2 = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %b1, i32 1000)
  %max3 = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %c1, i32 1000)
  %max4 = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %d1, i32 1000)
  
  store i32 %max1, i32* null
  store i32 %max2, i32* null
  store i32 %max3, i32* null
  store i32 %max4, i32* null
  ret void
}

; Test case 4: Mixed intrinsics to test type consistency
; RUN: opt -passes=slp-vectorizer -S -o -

define void @test_mixed_intrinsics(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c, i32 %d) {
entry:
  %a1 = and i32 %a, 32767  ; 15 bits max
  %b1 = and i32 %b, 32767
  %c1 = and i32 %c, 32767
  %d1 = and i32 %d, 32767
  
  ; These should all be reducible to 16-bit operations
  %abs1 = call i32 @llvm.abs.i32(i32 %a1, i1 true)
  %smin1 = call i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32 %b1, i32 10000)
  %smax1 = call i32 @llvm.smax.i32(i32 %c1, i32 -10000)
  %umax1 = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %d1, i32 5000)
  
  store i32 %abs1, i32* null
  store i32 %smin1, i32* null
  store i32 %smax1, i32* null
  store i32 %umax1, i32* null
  ret void
}

declare i32 @llvm.abs.i32(i32, i1)
declare i32 @llvm.smin.i32(i32, i32)
declare i32 @llvm.smax.i32(i32, i32)
declare i32 @llvm.umin.i32(i32, i32)
declare i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32, i32)
```

The main issues these tests would expose:
1. The `abs` intrinsic handling in `collectValuesToDemote` doesn't have proper bitwidth checking logic
2. Potential type mismatches when creating reduced-bitwidth vectors
3. Inconsistent handling of the second parameter for `abs` intrinsic when bitwidth is reduced

The patch needs to ensure that when reducing bitwidth for these intrinsics, the types remain consistent throughout the transformation, and all necessary checks are in place for each supported intrinsic type.
